rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES F THOMAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required per LRSF 14.3(A)(5) re petition for probate of lost will. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Rulings: grant without hearing

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JASHVANT M. PATEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/25/2020. 1) Proposed orders must be on mandatory Judicial Council forms (DE-226). 2) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues has not been filed since the previous tentative ruling: A) Attachment 7b was not attached, even though item 1b was checked. B) The petition must contain an allegation required by LRSF 14.24(B)(1) regarding the source of property. C) The names of all current, successor, and alternate trustees named in the devisee trust other than the petitioner must be identified. The proof of service (filed 07/15/2020) lists additional recipients of notice and their relationship to the decedent, but it does not identify the trustees or beneficiaries of the trust. D) All the assets listed on Attachment 7a appear to be distributable to the devisee trust. Why should the petitioner be deemed eligible for relief sought by the subject petition, even though Probate Code § 13501(b) requires probate administration of "property disposed of in trust under the decedent's will"?
